Timestamp Semantics: Value belongs to the 1-minute bar labeled by datetime and represents the closing price of that minute bucket.
T-1 Decision Rule: For a 10:00:00 decision, use the 09:59:00 close as the latest completed SPX minute state.
Notes: Chosen source of truth for spx_at_entry in target construction.

Timestamp Semantics: Timestamp is the opening time of the 1-minute bar; a 10:00:00 row represents the bar covering [10:00:00, 10:01:00).
T-1 Decision Rule: For a 10:00:00 decision, use the 09:59:00 completed bar.
Notes: Canonical SPX minute bar key.

Timestamp Semantics: Value belongs to the 1-minute bar labeled by datetime and represents the highest price in that minute bucket.
T-1 Decision Rule: For start-of-minute decisions, the current-minute high is not known yet; use the prior completed bar.
Notes: SPX minute OHLC source.

Timestamp Semantics: Value belongs to the 1-minute bar labeled by datetime and represents the lowest price in that minute bucket.
T-1 Decision Rule: For start-of-minute decisions, the current-minute low is not known yet; use the prior completed bar.
Notes: SPX minute OHLC source.

Timestamp Semantics: Value belongs to the 1-minute bar labeled by datetime and represents the first price in that minute bucket.
T-1 Decision Rule: Do not use the 10:00:00 bar open as T-1 state for a 10:00:00 decision; use the 09:59:00 bar instead.
Notes: SPX minute OHLC source.

Timestamp Semantics: Price snapshot fields belong to the labeled minute greek snapshot bucket for a specific option contract identified by date, strike, and right.
T-1 Decision Rule: For a start-of-minute decision, use the prior completed minute bucket ask snapshot.
Notes: Chosen intraday option ask source for v1.2 decision-state and target pricing.

Timestamp Semantics: Price snapshot fields belong to the labeled minute greek snapshot bucket for a specific option contract identified by date, strike, and right.

Timestamp Semantics: Timestamp labels the minute-level greek snapshot bucket used by ThetaData for interval=1m; a 10:00:00 row is treated as the greek/IV state for the 10:00 minute bucket.
T-1 Decision Rule: For a 10:00:00 decision, use the 09:59:00 greek snapshot as the latest completed greek state.
Notes: Rename timestamp to datetime in v1.2 decision-state build.

Timestamp Semantics: Date is the option expiration / trade date. OI for that date was set at the prior close and is available before market open on the labeled date.
T-1 Decision Rule: Same-day join for decision date D because the row dated D is already the usable OI snapshot for that trading day.
Notes: 0DTE OI raw source. Backward-looking by nature but operationally usable before the labeled session opens.
